round_history.fixes_landed_in: bd1fc42 — package.json files+=hooks/, shebangs cleaned, metadata test strengthened (attempt 1, didn't actually apply due to tool ordering), CHANGELOG/wizard-doc/README install-path lies fixed

3b15568 — metadata test strengthening actually landed this round (exact-line awk + diff fail path), README/TESTING.md '6 MATCH' → 10, CLAUDE_CODE_GDLC_WIZARD.md fallback wording fixed, wizard-doc canonical metadata-block alignment between outline + Case-study template
n/a (CERTIFIED). One minor doc precision noted (TESTING.md '2 .gitignore entries ≈ 10 rows' → '1 row covering 2 entries = 10 rows'); not a ship blocker per Codex; fixed in this commit anyway.

mission: Stand up BaseInfinity/claude-gdlc-wizard (Phase 1 per ~/gdlc/ROADMAP.md) as the Claude Code distribution repo for the 4 GDLC skills that graduated from ~/gdlc/. Consumers `npx claude-gdlc-wizard init` into their game projects to get: 4 skills (gdlc/setup/update/feedback), 2 enforcement hooks (prompt-check + instructions-loaded) + helper, a settings.json wired for $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR, and CLAUDE_CODE_GDLC_WIZARD.md at project root. Sibling ~/gdlc/ remains the playbook source of truth (decision recommendation per roadmap 1.4). The reference skeleton is BaseInfinity/claude-sdlc-wizard — layout parity is load-bearing because users run both wizards in parallel.
success: Reviewer verifies: (1) `node cli/bin/gdlc-wizard.js init` in a clean tmpdir creates exactly 9 files (settings.json + 3 hook files + 4 skills + wizard doc) + 2 .gitignore entries, and is fully idempotent on re-run; (2) CLI mode uses $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR and plugin mode uses ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT} — swapping either breaks installs, and test-plugin.sh::test_hooks_json_uses_plugin_root + test-cli.sh::test_settings_json_uses_project_dir enforce the split; (3) `_find-gdlc-root.sh` IS installed (unlike SDLC, where it's a silent CLI bug) — verify cli/init.js:21 lists it and test-cli.sh::test_cli_installs_from_source diffs it; (4) hook content is GDLC-specific with no SDLC residue (grep for 'SDLC BASELINE', 'setup-wizard', 'SDLC.md' in installed hooks should return nothing — test-cli.sh::test_hook_content_is_gdlc_specific); (5) gdlc-feedback skill uses stock GitHub labels (bug/enhancement/question) and the 5 canonical type identifiers as [<type>] title prefixes — test-skill-contracts.sh::test_feedback_uses_stock_labels_only + test_feedback_has_five_canonical_types; (6) the 5-line metadata block in skills/gdlc-setup/SKILL.md step-6 matches the template in CLAUDE_CODE_GDLC_WIZARD.md §Case-study GDLC.md template — test-skill-contracts.sh::test_setup_metadata_block_matches_wizard_doc; (7) install.sh has no stale 'agentic-sdlc-wizard' / 'claude-sdlc-wizard' references — test-install-script.sh::test_does_not_reference_sdlc_package; (8) all 94 tests pass when run as `git checkout -- hooks/ && for t in tests/*.sh; do bash "$t"; done` in one shell (the hooks/ wipe between separate Bash tool calls is an in-session environment artifact, not a product bug — confirm by running in a fresh clone of the repo where the wipe should not occur).
failure: Reviewer rubber-stamps 'tests pass' without checking: (a) that CLI-mode vs plugin-mode path prefixes haven't been swapped (if both use $CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT, plugin installs into a consumer project silently break because the var isn't set); (b) that `_find-gdlc-root.sh` really lands on disk after CLI init — grep cli/init.js for '_find-gdlc-root' and confirm it's in FILES; if missing, every installed hook crashes at source time but CI would pass because the sourcing only fails at hook runtime; (c) that the 5 canonical gdlc-feedback types include the exact strings 'earned-rule-candidate', 'playbook-gap', 'playbook-bug', 'wizard-bug', 'methodology-question' — short-form aliases (earned-rule/gap/bug/question) are documented but the canonical identifiers must be present; (d) that the 2 installed hooks exit 0 under ALL scenarios (GDLC.md present, empty, absent, outside-project) — any exit≠0 blocks the user's prompt submission; (e) that cli/init.js::mergeSettings does not clobber a user's existing settings.json that already has hooks for other plugins — feed it a JSON with {hooks:{UserPromptSubmit:[{hooks:[{command:'/path/other-plugin.sh'}]}]}} and confirm both entries are present after merge; (f) whether the scope cuts (dropped 3 SDLC hooks) are honestly documented in the preflight rather than hand-waved — especially precompact-seam-check, which interop-matters once someone runs both wizards together; (g) that the hooks/hooks.json and cli/templates/settings.json declare the SAME set of events — mismatch means plugin users and CLI users get different enforcement; (h) that the setup-wizard skill refuses to overwrite an existing GDLC.md body (ratchet history is sacred) — existing-GDLC-body test is the highest-value single contract.
verification_checklist: (a) Run `git checkout -- hooks/ && for t in tests/*.sh; do echo "--- $t"; bash "$t" || exit 1; done` from the repo root. Expect 94 passing assertions, 0 failures.

(b) cd /tmp/gdlc-verify-$$ && node /path/to/repo/cli/bin/gdlc-wizard.js init → expect 9 files created under .claude/ + CLAUDE_CODE_GDLC_WIZARD.md + .gitignore. Then run `gdlc-wizard check` → expect 6 MATCH rows and exit 0. Then `gdlc-wizard init` again → expect all SKIP, exit 0. Then `gdlc-wizard init --force` → expect OVERWRITE rows, exit 0.

(c) Matcher parity: the 2 events in hooks/hooks.json must match the 2 events in cli/templates/settings.json — compare with `jq -r '.hooks | keys' on both`. Currently: UserPromptSubmit, InstructionsLoaded.

(d) Path prefix parity: grep for '$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR' in hooks/hooks.json (should be EMPTY — plugin hooks use CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT). Grep for '$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT' in cli/templates/settings.json (should be EMPTY — CLI hooks use CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR).
(e) Plugin-source parity: run CLI init into a tmpdir, then `diff -q` each installed hook/skill against the repo source. Expect 0 diffs.
(f) Stock-labels rule: grep 'feedback:earned-rule\|feedback:playbook\|feedback:bug\|feedback:wizard' in skills/gdlc-feedback/SKILL.md → expect no hits (legacy custom labels are gone).

(g) 5 canonical type identifiers: grep these literal strings in skills/gdlc-feedback/SKILL.md: 'earned-rule-candidate', 'playbook-gap', 'playbook-bug', 'wizard-bug', 'methodology-question'. All 5 must be present.

(h) Metadata drift check: extract the 5 `<!-- GDLC ... -->` comment markers from skills/gdlc-setup/SKILL.md and from CLAUDE_CODE_GDLC_WIZARD.md §Case-study GDLC.md template. They must match exactly. Drift here means every consumer install gets an out-of-date stub.
(i) Install-script stale refs: grep 'agentic-sdlc-wizard\|claude-sdlc-wizard' in install.sh → expect no hits. Grep 'claude-gdlc-wizard' → expect at least one.

(j) Preflight honesty: open .reviews/preflight-phase-1.md §Intentional scope cuts. For each cut (dropped 3 SDLC hooks, live install gated, no scripts/, single workflow), confirm the rationale is concrete — not hand-waving. If a cut lacks a 'because' clause, push back.

(k) Known-good test: temporarily edit skills/gdlc-setup/SKILL.md to replace 'step-7' with 'step-8' and rerun test-skill-contracts.sh — test_setup_has_all_eight_steps should fail. Revert the edit. This is a liveness check that the contract tests actually test.

(l) CI workflow sanity: .github/workflows/ci.yml must iterate tests/*.sh AND install jq + xxd (the latter needed for tests/test-install-script.sh::test_shebang_no_escaped_bang). If either apt-get install is missing, CI will green-fail that test by skipping it.
review_instructions: Be strict. Two areas deserve extra scrutiny: (1) the CLI/plugin path-prefix split — swapping $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR vs ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT} is a silent breakage that tests today catch only by string-match (no functional test runs a real plugin install). (2) The _find-gdlc-root.sh installation bit — if it's missing from cli/init.js::FILES, every CLI-installed hook breaks at source time, but all the structural/content tests in the suite would still pass. Treat these as P0. Lower-priority items: scope-cut rationale honesty, README install docs accuracy, skill contract test liveness. This is a first-round handoff; flag concerns, don't paper over them.
